title: Stenoloma fumarioides
intro: Stenoloma fumarioides, also known as the False Helleborine, is a type of fern species belonging to the family Aspleniaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Stenoloma fumarioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as dried extracts and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory problems and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Stenoloma fumarioides is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The genus Stenoloma is part of the larger group of lindsaeoid ferns. Stenoloma fumarioides is further classified as a member of the genus Stenoloma, with its scientific classification being Stenoloma fumarioides. This classification places it within the broader family of Lindsaeaceae, which consists of tropical ferns.
uses: Unfortunately, I do not have information on the medicinal uses of Stenoloma fumarioides.
